rationale: The synthetic determinism check is fundamentally unprovable. det_maybe_ack_packet_impl_equal requires Vec<u8> equality (==) on the .id fields of dst and src for the Some/Some case. The available preconditions (maybe_ack_packet/send_ack + valid_ack) only give view-level (@/Seq) equality. Verus's SMT-level == on Vec<u8> is opaque: Vec is not Structural, has only obeys_view_eq but no obeys_concrete_eq, so v1@ == v2@ does NOT imply v1 == v2 (confirmed via multiple standalone tests including =~~= deep ext-equality). The case analysis on should_ack_single_message correctly forces (None,None) or (Some,Some), and the abstract-level facts (r1.unwrap()@.src/dst, msg view Ack with seqno) propagate to Seq-level id equality and the CSingleMessage::Ack u64 seqno equality. But the gap between Seq<u8>@ equality and Vec<u8> == cannot be closed without an extra axiom that Verus does not provide for Vec. A prior attempt with full case-splitting and broadcast group_vec_axioms / group_seq_axioms / group_se
